description_long: This object is a stylized flower with a prominent, thick green stem and two large, angular green leaves extending from its sides. The flower head features a central, pale yellow disc surrounded by eight broad, flat petals that are a vibrant shade of purple-pink. The petals are arranged radially and are evenly spaced, giving the flower a symmetrical, daisy-like appearance. The leaves are geometric and sharply faceted, with one leaf positioned higher and the other lower on the stem. The overall form is simple and low-poly, with clean edges and a matte finish. This flower would be visually striking in an urban park, garden, or streetscape planter.
description: A stylized purple-pink flower with a yellow center, thick green stem, and two large angular green leaves. The petals are broad, flat, and radially arranged.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the flower, with the yellow center and all petals clearly visible, and both leaves extending outward.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the left leaf more prominently and showing the petals' depth and layering.
description_view_2: Rear view with the petals fanned out, the yellow center partially visible, and the right leaf more in focus.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the right leaf and the radial symmetry of the petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, evenly spaced around the central disc, with no visible overlap or layering. The arrangement is highly symmetrical, resembling a typical daisy or aster pattern.
